source: Sed, Awk & Grep - Sed command: s///, backreferences, and the a/i/c/n commands

questions.q: What is the sed substitution syntax?
What does the trailing g do in s/old/new/g?
options: Replaces ALL matches on a line, not just the first
questions.why: Without g, only the first match on each line is replaced.

questions.q: In a replacement, what does & refer to?
options: The whole string that was matched
questions.why: & inserts the entire matched text into the replacement.

questions.q: In sed, what does \1 refer to?
options: The text captured by the first () group

questions.q: What do the a\text and i\text commands do?
options: a appends line(s) AFTER the matched line; i inserts line(s) BEFORE it
a inserts before; i appends after
questions.why: a = append after, i = insert before, c = change (replace) the line.

questions.q: How do you print ONLY the lines that were substituted?
options: Use the p flag on s together with -n, e.g. sed -n 's/a/b/p'
questions.why: -n suppresses auto-print and the p flag on s prints just the changed lines (the I flag would make the match case-insensitive).
